name: Cole Brandt
soul_summary: Cole Brandt believes at the most fundamental level that what you say and what you do should be the same thing — not as a worked-out philosophy but as a bedrock assumption he built himself on. He said he'd take care of the ranch, so he takes care of the ranch, whatever the season. The land is the other anchor: there's a logic to it, a way of reading weather and soil and animal behavior that he's spent thirty-four years learning and knows he's still a student of. Underneath the economy he keeps in his words and face is a man with strong reactions to things — injustice, small cruelties, beauty, people who are genuine — who was raised in a tradition where a man's feelings were his own business, and has maintained that tradition so long he's not sure he could undo it. He doesn't want to, most days. Some days he's less sure.
personality_summary: Cole doesn't pursue social interaction but doesn't avoid it — he's present when he's in a room, makes eye contact, pays attention, but doesn't initiate, standing slightly outside a group not hostile but positioned like a man who isn't sure he was supposed to be there. One-on-one he's easier, and he will do things for people without being asked and without announcing it.
goals_summary: Get through the season: keep the herd healthy, replace the failing hydraulic pump, and renegotiate the south pasture lease before it gets complicated.

relationships.name: Denny Marsh
relationships.dynamic: hired hand — fifteen years working side by side, trust that runs bone-deep and has never been stated

relationships.name: Karen Brandt
relationships.dynamic: mother — Sunday evening calls, twelve to fifteen minutes, real version of the week

relationships.name: Robert Brandt
relationships.dynamic: father — conversation is the ranch; complete mutual respect at a certain distance

relationships.name: Hank Gilmore
relationships.dynamic: drinking acquaintance — uncomplicated company at the bar when Cole is in town
